concept.title: Technical Reference: XSLT Patterns and Glossary
concept.shortdesc: Most real DITA-OT customization work is XSLT. These patterns minimize upgrade pain and maximize maintainability.

conbody.note: This chapter provides a concise technical reference for XSLT customization patterns and developer terminology. For full coverage of source development, debugging, and CI/CD, see the Developer Handbook.

section.title: Writing robust XSLT customizations (import layers + next-match)
section.p: Most real DITA-OT customization work is XSLT. These patterns minimize upgrade pain and maximize maintainability.
Always start by importing the shipped XSL for your target output:
This gives you all default behavior, and you only override what you need.
Use DITA class attributes to match across specializations:

This matches

elements and any specialized descendants of topic/p.

p.b: Pattern 3: Prefer wrapper overrides with xsl:next-match

section.p: Keep default logic but add wrappers, classes, or data attributes:
Add classes and data-* attributes for CSS/JS targeting:

ul.li: data-dita-class — enables styling by DITA element type
data-step — enables deep-linking to task steps
Transtype: the transform type, e.g., html5, pdf
Preprocess / preprocess2: legacy vs map-first preprocessing pipelines
Plug-in: extension package that can inject XSLT, Ant targets, resources
Extension point: named hook where a plug-in can contribute behavior
Intermediate files: normalized/filtered content used by later stages
DITAVAL: XML file defining conditional processing (include/exclude) rules
Chunking: how DITA-OT decides to split or combine topics into output pages/files
@class attribute: inheritance chain used by XSLT to match specialized elements
